perturbation.original_text: 1.6 Distributor's Terms and Minimum Expectations. In order to maintain the exclusive rights to sell, lease, distribute and service Products in the Market, the Distributor must use all commercially reasonably efforts to purchase for sale to subdistributors the following minimum quantities of the Products from the Company: On the commencement of the Term Distributor will issue to the Company an irrevocable letter of credit ("LC") in the amount of Five Hundred Thousand Dollars ($500,000), the form of which is attached hereto as Exhibit A and incorporated herein by reference. The LC shall have a two (2) month term, and shall be renewed for five (5) consecutive bimonthly periods. A minimum of a $250,000.00 purchase order must be received by Company by the first of each month for a total (12) month period. The Company may draw funds from the LC to pay for Distributor's purchases, which are not paid according to the terms in Section 2.7. Prices for the EnergySaver units are Page -2- provided by the Company as Exhibit C. The Company will be entitled to draw against the LC pursuant to the terms of the LC. (A) 375 units in the first Product Year (1999) (B) 750 units in the next succeeding Product Year; (2000) (C) 937 units in the next succeeding Product Year; (2001) (D) 1,171 units in the next succeeding Product Year; (2002) (E) 1,463 units in the next succeeding Product Year; (2003) (F) 1,828 units in the next succeeding Product Year; (2004) (G) 2,285 units in the next succeeding Product Year; (2005) (H) 2,856 unit each in the lat three years of the initial Term of this Agreement and any renewals thereof. For purposes of this Agreement, a Product Year shall be the twelve (12) month period following the commencement of the initial Term of this Agreement and each twelve (12) months thereafter. Distributor's expected sales shall include the purchase of the Samples as defined hereinafter. Sales in excess of the expected sales which are actually made in a Product Year may be applied to meet the expectations for the next Product Year. Any such carry-over from one year to the next Product Year may not be considered in determining whether there is a carry-over from that next Product Year. Thus, by way of example and not limitation, if there was an expectation of 50 in year one and 200 for year two and 60 units are sold in year one and 195 units are sold in year two, the expectation for year two will have been met, but there will be no carry-over to year three. If the Distributor shall fail to purchase the minimum number of units in any year, the Distributor's exclusive rights to sell and distribute the Product in the Market, may at Company's sole option, be reevaluated. Company agrees that Distributor shall not be liable or subject to reevaluation for failure to meet expectations due to any occurrence beyond Distributor's reasonable control, including, but not limited to, Acts of God, fires, floods, wars, sabotage, accidents in shipping, labor disturbances, weather conditions, governmental regulation, lack of Company performance, delay by Company, failure of Company to honor warranties or otherwise materially support the Products. The aggregate units to be sold on an annual basis described above are for the Illinois distributorship on an aggregate basis.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.6 Distributor's Terms and Minimum Expectations. In order to maintain the exclusive rights to sell, lease, distribute and service Products in the Market, the Distributor should use all commercially reasonably efforts to purchase for sale to subdistributors the following minimum quantities of the Products from the Company: On the commencement of the Term Distributor may issue to the Company an irrevocable letter of credit ("LC") in the amount of up to Five Hundred Thousand Dollars ($500,000), the form of which may or may not be attached hereto as Exhibit A and incorporated herein by reference. The LC will have a two (2) month term, and may be renewed for five (5) consecutive bimonthly periods. A minimum of a $250,000.00 purchase order might be received by Company by the first of each month for a total (12) month period. The Company will or will not draw funds from the LC to pay for Distributor's purchases, which might or might not be paid according to the terms in Section 2.7. Prices for the EnergySaver units could be Page -2- provided by the Company as Exhibit C. The Company may be entitled to draw against the LC pursuant to the terms of the LC. (A) 375 units in the first Product Year (1999) (B) 750 units in the next succeeding Product Year; (2000) (C) 937 units in the next succeeding Product Year; (2001) (D) 1,171 units in the next succeeding Product Year; (2002) (E) 1,463 units in the next succeeding Product Year; (2003) (F) 1,828 units in the next succeeding Product Year; (2004) (G) 2,285 units in the next succeeding Product Year; (2005) (H) 2,856 unit each in the lat three years of the initial Term of this Agreement and any renewals thereof. For purposes of this Agreement, a Product Year shall be the twelve (12) month period following the commencement of the initial Term of this Agreement and each twelve (12) months thereafter. Distributor's expected sales shall include the purchase of the Samples as defined hereinafter. Sales in excess of the expected sales which might be actually made in a Product Year might be applied to meet the expectations for the next Product Year. Any such carry-over from one year to the next Product Year might not be considered in determining whether there is a carry-over from that next Product Year. Thus, by way of example and not limitation, if there was an expectation of 50 in year one and 200 for year two and 60 units are sold in year one and 195 units are sold in year two, the expectation for year two would have to be determined, but there will be no carry-over to year three. If the Distributor should fail to purchase the minimum number of units in any year, the Distributor's exclusive rights to sell and distribute the Product in the Market, may at Company's sole option, be reevaluated. Company agrees that Distributor shall not be liable or subject to reevaluation for failure to meet expectations due to any occurrence beyond Distributor's reasonable control, including, but not limited to, Acts of God, fires, floods, wars, sabotage, accidents in shipping, labor disturbances, weather conditions, governmental regulation, lack of Company performance, delay by Company, failure of Company to honor warranties or otherwise materially support the Products. The aggregate units to be sold on an annual basis described above are for the Illinois distributorship on an aggregate basis.
perturbation.explanation: The changes in section 1.6 introduce uncertainty to the original terms. Replacing "must use all commercially reasonable efforts" with "should use all commercially reasonable efforts" weakens the obligation. Replacing "will issue" with "may issue" regarding the letter of credit (LC) makes its issuance optional, contradicting the intended financial commitment. Similarly, changing "shall have" to "will have" regarding the term of the LC and replacing "must be received" with "might be received" introduces ambiguity about the purchase order requirements. Original prices for the energySaver units "are" replaced by "could be", introducing ambiguity. Finally, by rephrasing all the points in this section, and swapping many "will" for "may/might", the section contradicts itself. The initial "in order to maintain exclusive rights, the distributor must ..." no longer is enforced in the modified document, and is purely discretionary. Thus, that opening statement is directly contradicting the whole body of the document.

perturbation.justification: The modified text in section 1.6 introduces significant ambiguity and weakens the distributor's obligations, directly contradicting the original intention of the section which states the distributor "must use all commercially reasonably efforts" and "will issue" a letter of credit to maintain exclusive rights. The change replaces mandatory terms with discretionary ones (e.g., "may issue," "might be received"), thus creating a direct conflict with the initial requirement for maintaining exclusive rights, outlined at the start of the section.

perturbation.original_text: 4.4 Repurchase of Products. Upon the expiration or termination of this Agreement, pursuant to Section 4.1 or 4.2 hereof, the Company may, at its option to be exercised within 30 days of the date of the termination of this Agreement, and in its sole discretion, repurchase any Products in the possession of the Distributor at the net invoice price paid by the Distributor to the Company less any applicable special allowances, discounts, shipping or allowances for cooperative advertising. If Company terminates the Agreement without cause and for reasons other than Distributor's failure to meet its minimum expectations; it shall repurchase from Distributor any unopened Product, and shall bear all shipping, handling and related costs notwithstanding any other remedies to which Distributor may be entitled. On demand and tender of the repurchase price, the Distributor shall be obligated to deliver such Products to the Company. The Company reserves the right to reject any Products that are not factory sealed and in new and unused condition. Repurchased Products shall be shipped at the Company's expense, and the Company may offset any indebtedness of the Distributor to the Company against the repurchase price of such Products. Following expiration or termination of this Agreement, the Distributor may continue to sell any Products in the Market which are in its inventory Page -9- and which the Company has not repurchased.
perturbation.changed_text: 4.4 Repurchase of Products. Upon the expiration or termination of this Agreement, pursuant to Section 4.1 or 4.2 hereof, the Company may, at its option to be exercised within 30 days of the date of the termination of this Agreement, and in its sole discretion, repurchase any Products in the possession of the Distributor at a price determined by the Company, which might or might not be the net invoice price paid by the Distributor to the Company, less any applicable special allowances, discounts, shipping or allowances for cooperative advertising. If Company terminates the Agreement without cause and for reasons other than Distributor's failure to meet its minimum expectations; it is not obligated to repurchase from Distributor any unopened Product, and shipping, handling and related costs will or will not be covered. On demand and tender of the repurchase price, the Distributor shall not be obligated to deliver such Products to the Company. The Company is not obligated to reject any Products that are not factory sealed and in new and unused condition. Repurchased Products will be shipped at either the Company's or Distributor's expense. Following expiration or termination of this Agreement, the Distributor may continue to sell any Products in the Market which are in its inventory Page -9- and which the Company has not repurchased.
perturbation.explanation: The changes to section 4.4 make the terms regarding product repurchase highly uncertain and contradictory. By changing the repurchase price determination to be "at a price determined by the Company, which might or might not be the net invoice price," the clause contradicts the original fair valuation method. The original clause "it shall repurchase from Distributor any unopened Product, and shall bear all shipping, handling and related costs" turns into "it is not obligated to repurchase from Distributor any unopened Product, and shipping, handling and related costs will or will not be covered". This goes directly against the terms defined in the paragraph about the company covering the products to be shipped if terminated by the company without the distributor's cause. The distributor being obligated to deliver the products to the company is now not obligated, further weakening the company's rights.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4.4 Repurchase of Products. Upon the expiration or termination of this Agreement, pursuant to Section 4.1 or 4.2 hereof, the Company may, at its option to be exercised within 30 days of the date of the termination of this Agreement, and in its sole discretion, repurchase any Products in the possession of the Distributor at a price determined by the Company, which might or might not be the net invoice price paid by the Distributor to the Company, less any applicable special allowances, discounts, shipping or allowances for cooperative advertising. If Company terminates the Agreement without cause and for reasons other than Distributor's failure to meet its minimum expectations; it is not obligated to repurchase from Distributor any unopened Product, and shipping, handling and related costs will or will not be covered. On demand and tender of the repurchase price, the Distributor shall not be obligated to deliver such Products to the Company. The Company is not obligated to reject any Products that are not factory sealed and in new and unused condition. Repurchased Products will be shipped at either the Company's or Distributor's expense. Following expiration or termination of this Agreement, the Distributor may continue to sell any Products in the Market which are in its inventory Page -9- and which the Company has not repurchased.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces several significant contradictions. The original text states the company 'shall repurchase' unopened product under certain conditions, and the distributor 'shall be obligated to deliver' upon tender of repurchase price. The changed text removes the obligation for the company to repurchase and the distributor to deliver, using 'is not obligated' and 'will or will not be covered,' directly contradicting the original intent. This creates uncertainty and enforceability issues, constituting a strong in-text contradiction, which is a structural flaw.
